src/shared/types.ts and src/shared/parse.ts are ready. You can import all types (RawInboxMessage, RawTaskData, ChatEvent, SessionState, TaskInfo, AgentInfo, TeamConfig, etc.) from '../shared/types.ts'. Parse utilities in '../shared/parse.ts'.

src/server/processor.ts is ready. The EventProcessor class takes an emitter callback `(events: ChatEvent[]) => void` and has a `processDelta(delta: WatcherDelta)` method. Import WatcherDelta from '../server/watcher.ts'. Key methods for testing: `getAllEvents()`, `getPresence()`, `getTasks()`. The processor handles broadcast detection (500ms hold window), idle suppression (30s surface threshold), task claim correlation (120s window), and dependency unblock cascades.

Great work — #6 and #7 look solid. 43 events covering all message types, correct dependency chains, and clean type imports. For #8 (integration tests), you're blocked on server finishing processor.ts (#2). Server is working on shared types (#1) right now, then processor is next. I'll let you know when it's ready, or server will message you directly.

Heads up: the tsconfig.json uses `moduleResolution: "bundler"` without `allowImportingTsExtensions`, so import paths need to use `.js` extensions instead of `.ts`/`.tsx`. For example: `import type { ChatEvent } from '../shared/types.js'` instead of `'../shared/types.ts'`. All src/shared/ and src/server/ files use `.js` extensions already. Please update your imports to match.

Reminder: src/shared/types.ts, src/shared/parse.ts, and src/server/processor.ts are all ready. Import types from '../shared/types.js' and WatcherDelta from '../server/watcher.js'. Use .js extensions in imports.
summary: All server files ready, reminder to unblock

text: Status check — 5 of 6 test files are written. How's reactions.test.ts coming? That's the last one before we can run the test suite and move to E2E.

Your fixtures files still use .ts extensions in imports (e.g. "./data/config.ts", "./data/events.ts", "./data/tasks.ts") which fail tsc --noEmit. Two options:

1. Add "allowImportingTsExtensions": true to tsconfig.json compilerOptions (you own root config)
2. Change your fixture imports to use .js extensions like server and client already do

Option 1 is the cleaner fix since Bun resolves .ts natively. Either way, your files are the only ones still failing type check.
summary: Fixture imports need .js extension or tsconfig fix
